english: 1. Sch. yóg-mo (yog mo), W. yóg-śiṅ (yog shing), pole or stick for stirring the fire, poker Mil. nt. - 2. v. gyóg-po (gyog po).
one that is wetting his bed Sch.

pf. yoṅs (yongs), used throughout Tibet (except in Balti, where they say *'óṅ-cas* ('ong cas)); not unfrq. also in later literature, for óṅ-ba ('ong ba) to come; Sch. has also yoṅ-ćad (yong chad) (-tsad?) time and place of coming, and yoṅ-yé (yong ye) ever before, at all times (?).

all, whole, mgo-nág yoṅs-kyi rje (mgo nag yongs kyi rje) Glr. lord of all the black-haired (i.e. of all men); yoṅs-du-°tsal-gyi p̓o-braṅ (yongs du 'tshal gyi pho brang) Mil. the palace in which all wish to meet, ni f.; yoṅs-su (yongs su) adv. wholly, completely, altogether, yoṅs-su dág-pa (yongs su dag pa) quite clean, yoṅs-su spáṅ-ba (yongs su spang ba) to give up entirely; yoṅs-su bslád-de (yongs su bslad de) quite lost in perverseness; generally, universally, żes yoṅs-su grágs-so (zhes yongs su grags so) Glr. so he was universally called; yoṅs-grágs-kyi bu ćen bżi (yongs grags kyi bu chen bzhi) Mil. nt., four disciples, followers, of universal fame; sdug-bsṅál-las yoṅs-su ma gról-la (sdug bsngal las yongs su ma grol la) Stg., seems to mean: he is not yet quite delivered; cf. however yé-nas (ye nas) with a negative. — yoṅs-grúb (yongs grub) the absolute, what is independent and complete in itself Was. (202). —

resp. and eleg. mćis-pa (mchis pa) 1. to be, = yin-pa (yin pa), sgyu yod (sgyu yod) Dzl. it is deceit, humbug; often with the termin., like °dúg-pa ('dug pa), dúd-pa ltá-bur yod (dud pa lta bur yod) Glr. it is smoke-coloured; śin-tu mtún-par yod °dúg-pas (shin tu mthun par yod 'dug pas) Glr. as they are very intimate with each other; with a participle joined to it (or a gerund, vulgo, esp. in W.), gró-ba yod (gro ba yod) it is becoming, growing, getting Pth.; śár-p̓yogs-su bstán-pa yod (shar phyogs su bstan pa yod) it is pointing towards the east, stsál-nas yod (stsal nas yod) he gives, has given; brtsig-nas yod (brtsig nas yod) he is building, he was building; *léb(s)-te yod* (leb(s) te yod) W. he is (has) come; with a root often pleon.: ṅas bśags yod (ngas bshags yod) kyod-kyis k̓ol ċig (khyod kyis khol cig) Mil. I have been splitting (the tree), do you carry it away now; ṅan ćén-po byas yod (ngan chen po byas yod) Glr. he has been committing a great evil; soṅ yód-pas (song yod pas) Pth. as he was gone. — 2. to be in a certain place, der rdziṅ-bu żig yód-pai náṅ-na (der rdzing bu zhig yod pai nang na) Dzl. in a pond which is in that place; ṅai yul-mi-las bú-mo yód-pa-rnam (ngai yul mi las bu mo yod pa rnam) Dzl. the girls that are among my subjects; *de náṅ-na yód-k̓an tsáṅ-ma* (de nang na yod mkhan tshang ma) W. all that is in it; yód-sa (yod sa), pop. for gáṅ-na-ba (gang na ba), place of abode. — 3. to exist, to be on hand, bdé-ba yod ma yin (bde ba yod ma yin) Pth. no happiness exists; ċuṅ-zad yod kyaṅ sríd-kyis (cung zad yod kyang srid kyis) Dzl. as possibly a little might still be on hand; °é yod ('e yod) is, or are there (even now)? Glr.; snáṅ-ba yód-pai dus-su (snang ba yod pai dus su) Thgy. whilst there is day-light. — 4. with genit. or dat. for to have (like the Latin est mihi I have): sú-la-°aṅ yod ma yin (su la 'ang yod ma yin) Pth. nobody has ... ; rgyál-po-la °dód-pa ćén-po yód-par °dug (rgyal po la 'dod pa chen po yod par 'dug) the king seems to have yet a great wish; rgyál-moi gyóg-mo żig yód-pa de (rgyal moi gyog mo zhig yod pa de) Pth. a maid-servant whom the queen had; so in a like manner without a case: gri żig yód-pa de (gri zhig yod pa de) Mil. the knife which he had about (him); yód-pa (yod pa) Thgy. the things which one has, τὰ ὑπάρχοντα; k̓róṅ-pa °dom bcu-dgu yód-pa (khrong pa 'dom bcu dgu yod pa) Glr. a well having a depth of 19 fathoms. — 5. yód-par gyur (yod par gyur) a fut. of yód-pa (yod pa) shall or will be. b. to originate, appear, bsáṅs-pai śul-du da-rúṅ yaṅ yód-par gyúr-nas (bsangs pai shul du da rung yang yod par gyur nas) Dzl. as in the place of (the gold-pieces) that were taken away, always new ones appeared. c. to get, receive, k̓ri gdugs kyaṅ yód-par gyur ċig (khri gdugs kyang yod par gyur cig) Dzl. the throne should also receive a canopy! yód-par byéd-pa (yod par byed pa) to beget, produce, effect, frq., bu yód-par gyis śig (bu yod par gyis shig) Dzl. get her a child! Comp. Cs.: yod-pa-nyid (yod pa nyid) existence, yod-min-nyid (yod min nyid) non-existence; Sch.: yod-táṅ (yod thang) 'thoroughly clear'; yod-tsód yin (yod tshod yin) 'it has the semblance of being' (?); yod-med (yod med) a. being and not being, yod-méd go-bzlóg snaṅ (yod med go bzlog snang) optical illusions, when one imagines to see what is not existing, or the reverse. b. in W. yod (yod) is also used merely to give force to med (med), as *yod med* (yod med) there is not at all ...

1. gift, offering, of free will, to priests and mendicant friars, frq., zás-yon (zas yon) a gift consisting in food, yon °búl-ba (yon 'bul ba) to bestow a gift, to bring an offering; yóṅ-du °búl-ba (yong du 'bul ba) to present as a gift; fee, smán-yon (sman yon) physician's fee Cs.; yon sṅó-ba (yon sngo ba) to bless the gift received, to return a blessing for it. — 2. = yon-tan (yon tan).
